skill.name: frontend-mobile-development-component-scaffold
skill.description: Scaffold production-ready React and React Native components with TypeScript, styling, accessibility guidance, tests, and Storybook examples.
skill.summary: Generate structured React and React Native component scaffolds with tests, styles, and documentation.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command and weak cryptography patterns, but review found Markdown fences and TypeScript template literals rather than executable shell or crypto logic. No prompt injection, network access, credential handling, or malicious intent was found. The only substantive concern is low risk: examples interpolate user-derived component metadata into generated code and should be validated by the agent before use.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown and TypeScript Backticks Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The reported external command locations are Markdown code fences or TypeScript template literals used to show generated React files. No shell commands, subprocess calls, or Ruby backtick execution were found in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are inside fenced TypeScript examples and string templates. I found no executable shell command pattern in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Matches
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography locations contain frontmatter text, property descriptions, type checks, and generated Storybook metadata. No MD5, SHA1, DES, password hashing, or cryptographic API usage was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line context does not contain cryptographic operations. The matches appear to come from short substrings in ordinary words or template code.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Array Join Used for Code Assembly
low_findings.description: Line 134 uses join('') to concatenate generated state hook snippets. This is ordinary code assembly in an example and is not obfuscation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding code maps state definitions into readable generated source. There is no hidden payload or decoding behavior.

low_findings.title: User-Derived Identifiers Interpolated Into Generated Code Examples
low_findings.description: The examples place component names, prop names, descriptions, and defaults into generated TypeScript, JSX, tests, and Storybook stories. This is expected for scaffolding, but agents should validate identifiers and escape descriptions before writing files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The interpolation is visible in the examples, but the file is instructional rather than executable. Risk depends on how directly an agent applies untrusted user input.

dangerous_patterns.title: Generated Source Interpolation Without Explicit Validation Step
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill demonstrates generating source code from user-supplied component specifications, but it does not explicitly require validation of component names, prop names, default values, or descriptions before interpolation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is present in code examples, but no executable scaffold script is shipped. This remains a low-risk guidance issue.

content.value_statement: Teams often lose time creating consistent React and React Native component structure by hand. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to produce typed components, styles, tests, and stories from a clear specification.
actual_capabilities: Defines a component specification format for props, state, hooks, styling, and platform targets.
Guides generation of React functional components with TypeScript interfaces and accessibility props.
Provides React Native component patterns using View, Text, StyleSheet, and accessibility labels.
Includes test generation guidance for rendering, events, and accessibility checks.
Provides CSS Modules, styled-components, Tailwind, and Storybook scaffolding examples.
limitations: It is an instructional skill, not a runnable package or command-line scaffold tool.
Generated examples may need project-specific imports, lint rules, and test setup changes.
It does not validate user-provided component names or prop names by itself.
It does not inspect an existing design system unless the user provides that context.

use_cases.title: Create a New Design System Component
use_cases.description: Generate a typed React component with props, styles, tests, and Storybook stories from a short product requirement.

use_cases.title: Build a React Native Screen Element
use_cases.description: Create a mobile component scaffold with native primitives, accessibility labels, and StyleSheet structure.

use_cases.title: Standardize Component Deliverables
use_cases.description: Use a repeatable output checklist so components include implementation, types, tests, stories, and exports.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Component Scaffold
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a single typed React component.
prompt_templates.prompt: Scaffold a React TypeScript Button component with label, variant, disabled, and onPress props. Include CSS Modules, tests, and a Storybook story.

prompt_templates.title: React Native Component
prompt_templates.scenario: Mobile component request with accessibility requirements.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a React Native Card component with title, body, imageUrl, and onPress props. Use StyleSheet and include accessible labels.

prompt_templates.title: Design System Pattern
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request aligned to a shared component library.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a reusable Input component for our design system. Include controlled state, validation message support, ARIA attributes, tests, and Storybook controls.

prompt_templates.title: Universal Component Scaffold
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for web and native variants.
prompt_templates.prompt: Scaffold a universal ProfileHeader component for React web and React Native. Provide shared TypeScript types, platform-specific implementations, tests, stories, and index exports.

output_examples.input: A request for a React TypeScript alert banner with variant, title, message, and dismiss action.
output: A component implementation plan with typed props and accessible roles.
Separate style guidance for the selected styling approach.
Test cases for rendering, dismiss behavior, and accessibility.
A Storybook story set with default and interactive examples.

output_examples.input: A request for a React Native list item with title, subtitle, icon, and press handling.
output: A native component scaffold using View, Text, TouchableOpacity, and StyleSheet.
Type definitions for display and interaction props.
Accessibility labels and role guidance for mobile screen readers.

output_examples.input: A request to standardize component output for a team library.
output_examples.output: The skill returns a consistent package of component file guidance, TypeScript types, styles, tests, Storybook stories, and barrel export instructions.

best_practices: Provide naming rules, prop definitions, styling choice, and platform target before asking for a scaffold.
Ask the agent to match existing project imports, folder structure, and test utilities.
Review generated identifiers, defaults, and descriptions before committing generated files.
anti_patterns: Do not request vague components without props, states, or interaction requirements.
Do not accept generated code without checking accessibility behavior and test assumptions.
Do not mix web and native APIs in one implementation unless a universal pattern is planned.

faq.question: Does this skill run commands or create files automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides scaffolding guidance and code-generation patterns for an AI coding assistant.

faq.question: Can it generate React Native components?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes React Native examples using common native primitives and StyleSheet.

faq.question: Does it support TypeScript?
faq.answer: Yes. The examples emphasize TypeScript interfaces and typed component props.

faq.question: Can it create tests?
faq.answer: Yes. It guides generation of render, interaction, and accessibility test cases.

faq.question: Does it know my project structure?
faq.answer: No. Provide your folder conventions, import aliases, styling system, and test setup for accurate output.

faq.question: Is it safe for marketplace publication?
faq.answer: Yes. Review found no executable commands, network access, credential handling, or malicious prompt injection.
